topic: SAA Formation ~950 AD (theological match to Revelation 20)
change_summary: tldr_evidence_length shrank 299→229 (-23.4%); rewrite from ISS-1887 fix (replaced Tower-of-Babel referent with Revelation-20/Satan-little-season and dropped unsupported millions-of-years clause). Verdict unchanged (Unfalsifiable). Sourced from drift-audit.json generated 2026-05-28T01:09Z; obe_skipped=4 (WIN-006/004/029/066 already re-reviewed since audit).
our_argument_summary: WIN-023 claims ~950 AD SAA formation matches Satan-little-season ~1000 AD. Our review verdict (Unfalsifiable) rests on three legs: (1) the ~950 AD date is post-hoc — taken from Campuzano 2019 SHAWQ2k paleomagnetic reconstruction, not derived from dome geometry; (2) Satan-little-season is not a date-anchored prophecy (the 1000-year reign of Revelation 20 has no fixed start in scripture), so the ~50yr offset is within any reasonable definitional band; (3) categorical/theological assertions have no observable that distinguishes a true-world from a false-world. Code_analysis tags: monitoring=none (no monitor.py domain), post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false, relabels_standard=false.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: None — WIN-023 has no monitor.py domain. The dome simply asserts CONFIRMED in 02-wins.txt with the parenthetical (matches Satan-little-season ~1000 AD).
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Neither — there is no comparison at all. The CONFIRMED label is self-awarded without any automated check.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None applicable — there is no validation code to evaluate tolerances against.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. detail_verdict_text correctly states monitor.py contains no validation domain; code_analysis.monitoring is now correctly tagged none (was hardcoded; fixed by ISS-1888 on 2026-05-08).

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: grep -ni "SAA|950 AD|win_023" raw-text/monitor.py returns zero matches. The WIN exists only as a webpage assertion.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard paleomagnetism (Campuzano 2019; Hare 2018) explains the ~950 AD reversed-flux patch via core-mantle boundary heat flux heterogeneity associated with the African LLSVP. The dome does not propose a competing numerical mechanism — it links the date to a theological timeline. This is theological reframing, not standard-physics relabeling. False is correct.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Campuzano et al. 2019 SHAWQ2k (Earth Planet Sci Lett, doi:10.1016/j.epsl.2019.01.050) was published before the dome model added WIN-023. The date was adopted, not predicted.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No formula in any raw-text file derives ~950 AD from disc_radius, firmament_height, sun_altitude, or moon_altitude. The dome’s own 06-evolution.txt admits failure to mathematically reconcile SAA splitting.

holes_found.description: WRONG DOI ON CAMPUZANO 2019 CITATION. detail_evidence cites "Campuzano et al. (2019, SHAWQ2k)" with link target https://doi.org/10.1016/j.epsl.2019.01.046, but that DOI resolves to a completely different paper: Kuchar et al. (2019), "The importance of lateral Earth structure for North American glacial isostatic adjustment," Earth and Planetary Science Letters. The correct DOI for the Campuzano SHAWQ2k paper is 10.1016/j.epsl.2019.01.050 (verified via Crossref: title "Emergence and evolution of the South Atlantic Anomaly revealed by the new paleomagnetic reconstruction SHAWQ2k", first author Campuzano, EPSL 2019). Off by 4 in the article-number — clearly a transcription error. A reader who clicks the link lands on glacial-isostatic-adjustment research, not the SAA reconstruction we claim to be citing. This is the exact failure mode flagged in CLAUDE.md ("Wrong DOIs have already been found in this review (WIN-008, 009, 014)"). A dome defender would seize on this — the citation is the load-bearing source for our post-hoc verdict, and the link contradicts itself. Per the prompt’s severity guideline, fabricated/wrong citations are critical because they let a defender discredit the entire review.
holes_found.recommendation: In data/wins.json, WIN-023 detail_evidence, replace https://doi.org/10.1016/j.epsl.2019.01.046 with https://doi.org/10.1016/j.epsl.2019.01.050. The display text "Campuzano et al. (2019, SHAWQ2k)" stays — only the DOI changes. Also propose a verifier script (or extend test.js) to crossref-validate every DOI in wins.json against api.crossref.org once per build; this is the third such bug and the class needs an automated fence.

holes_found.description: DEFENSE NEUTRALIZATION GAP — kernel-of-truth coverage on the theological-anchor specifics. Our detail_verdict_text dismisses the theological link as definitionally unfalsifiable but does not say WHY the date-anchor itself is loose. A close reader could counter "Revelation 20:7 says 1000 years and the SAA formed at 950 AD — that is a 5% miss, why is that not impressive?" The unstated answer is that "1000 years" in Revelation 20 has no fixed scriptural start (resurrection ~AD33, Constantine AD312, Council of Nicaea AD325, Theodosius AD380 are all candidate t=0s under different preterist readings), so the ~1000 AD target band is at minimum ±200yr wide — wider than the gap. Adding one sentence makes the verdict bulletproof without changing it.
holes_found.recommendation: Append to detail_evidence (or detail_verdict_text) a short clause noting that the theological t=0 anchor is itself non-unique: a 1000-year reign starting at the resurrection (AD33), Constantine (AD312), or Theodosius (AD380) yields target dates ranging ~AD1033 to ~AD1380, a band ~350yr wide. Combined with Hare 2018 recurrence intervals of 200-400yr, hitting some recurrence inside the band was statistically near-certain. Minor severity; current text already conveys the conclusion, this just hardens it against a sophisticated objector.

stronger_arguments.description: WIN-023 is a textbook example of the dome’s post-hoc retrodiction pattern, AND it is the only theological-anchor WIN that survives close inspection without an obvious factual error (compare WIN-031, where the six-text convergence collapses on Ancient-Near-East Zaphon-Zion analysis). This makes WIN-023 a useful pedagogical case in Part 3.1b (post-hoc standard): a claim that fails post-hoc analysis cleanly, without needing additional debunking on the science side. Consider adding WIN-023 as a worked example in Part 3.1b.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome correctly identifies that ~950 AD is a real archaeomagnetic event — Campuzano 2019 SHAWQ2k (data/wins.json:WIN-023.detail_evidence) and Tarduno 2015 (10.1038/ncomms8865) both document the emergence of the current reversed-flux patch in the late first millennium. The 50-year offset to a millennial theological target is also not coincidence-rejecting: archaeomagnetic dating uncertainty is ±50-100yr (data/wins.json:WIN-023.detail_evidence), so the dome’s offset is within instrument resolution.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The match is not from dome physics — the date is adopted from published literature, not derived (data/wins.json:WIN-023.code_analysis.derives_from_dome=false). The theological anchor (Revelation 20’s 1000-year reign) has no fixed scriptural start, so the target band is at minimum ±200yr wide, and Hare 2018 (10.1002/2017GL076007) documents archaeomagnetic SAA-like episodes spanning 425-1550 CE — multiple candidate matches within any millennial window were available. The dome’s own 06-evolution.txt admits "completely failed to mathematically reconcile SAA splitting", confirming the WIN is not a derivation, it is a pattern-match (raw-text/06-evolution.txt:16).

deeper_analysis: The c3 review caught the Tower-of-Babel/Revelation-20 referent error (ISS-1887) and the hardcoded/none monitoring tag error (ISS-1888); both are patched and the patches are correct in current data (confirmed by re-read after git pull --rebase at write time). The drift signal that flagged this WIN was the TLDR shortening from the ISS-1887 patch — expected, not adversarial. The new finding (wrong Campuzano DOI) is independent of the change that triggered the review; it has been present in detail_evidence since at least cycle 3 (the c3 review did not check the DOI against Crossref). It was caught this cycle only because Lens C / quantitative_verification expanded into citation-check via api.crossref.org. Recommendation: standardize a crossref-validate-all-DOIs check into test.js so future curmudgeon cycles don’t need to discover this manually one WIN at a time.

citations_verified: doi:10.1038/ncomms8865 — Tarduno et al. 2015 "Antiquity of the South Atlantic Anomaly and evidence for top-down control on the geodynamo", Nature Communications. Title matches; supports the ‘ancient SAA-like behavior’ framing as cited.

doi:10.1002/2017GL076007 — Hare et al. 2018 "New Archeomagnetic Directional Records From Iron Age Southern Africa (ca. 425–1550 CE) and Implications for the South Atlantic Anomaly", Geophysical Research Letters. Title matches; covers recurrence range. The dome review cites a 400-700 AD sub-window, which is plausible but I have not independently verified that Hare’s paper specifically isolates that sub-episode (paywall).
citations_failed: doi:10.1016/j.epsl.2019.01.046 — cited as Campuzano et al. 2019 SHAWQ2k, but Crossref returns ‘Kuchar et al. (2019) The importance of lateral Earth structure for North American glacial isostatic adjustment’. WRONG DOI. Correct DOI is 10.1016/j.epsl.2019.01.050 (Crossref-confirmed: Campuzano, EPSL 2019, ‘Emergence and evolution of the South Atlantic Anomaly revealed by the new paleomagnetic reconstruction SHAWQ2k’).

advocate_mode.best_defense: A theological-framework prediction does not need to derive numerical values from physics — it operates at a different ontological level. The Revelation 20 millennial reign points to a major spiritual transition ~AD1000, the dome model embeds that transition in cosmological geometry, and one of the most dramatic geomagnetic events of the late first millennium occurred at AD950 — a 50-year fit that is tight against either Hare 2018 archaeomagnetic recurrence cycles (200-400yr) or geological time. Even granting the dome did not derive the date from disc geometry, the cosmological framework’s broader coherence is supported by this match, just as a successful frequentist coincidence supports a theoretical generator. Your ‘unfalsifiable’ verdict is overreach: the prediction is falsifiable in principle — if archaeomagnetic data had shown the African LLSVP-driven event began ~AD500 or ~AD1500, the millennial-match would have failed. It did not fail; therefore the dome got something right, and you should grade it on what it claims to be (a theological-cosmological framework, not a physics derivation).
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Add a short clause to detail_verdict_text noting that the theological t=0 anchor is itself non-unique (resurrection AD33 vs Constantine AD312 vs Theodosius AD380 yields a target band ~AD1033-AD1380, ~350yr wide), so the apparent ~50yr fit is an artifact of selective reading. This makes the unfalsifiability verdict survive the ‘falsifiable in principle’ defense — the t=0 looseness combined with the Hare 2018 recurrence cadence means the dome could always find SOME archaeomagnetic event inside its target band, no matter where the real event lay.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: No contradictions. WIN-022 (Misleading) and WIN-024 (Misleading) attack SAA-related claims on the science side (duplicate of WIN-007; latitude coincidence). WIN-031 (Unfalsifiable) attacks a theological-convergence WIN with the same logic as WIN-023 — theological match without derivation from dome geometry. Our framing across these four WINs is consistent: science-side claims about SAA get science-side debunks, theological-anchor claims about SAA/north-pole get the Unfalsifiable verdict for the same structural reason. A defender could not productively compile a cross-WIN ‘your review contradicts itself’ attack.

claims_checked: tldr_evidence_length, tldr_verdict_length, detail_*_length recomputation against drift-audit fingerprint
quantitative_verification.computations: For each cited DOI, fetched api.crossref.org/works/<doi> and compared returned title/author/year against the dome-review citation. Campuzano DOI failed; other two passed. For lengths, computed s.length on the four fields in wins.json and confirmed they match drift-audit current_fingerprint exactly (claim 21, finding 339, detail_evidence 1527, detail_verdict 1329, tldr_evidence 229, tldr_verdict 130). No mathematical claims in the WIN itself to compute against.
